abstract.shortdesc: Aligns the selected solid body along the Z axis using its dominant revolution axis.
abstract.p: Clusters all cylindrical and conical faces by axis direction, selects the longest coaxial axis, and aligns accordingly. Use this as the first step when creating a new project.

abstract.shortdesc: Rotates the entire document 180° around the Y axis through the world origin.
abstract.p: Use this as the first step when creating a new project if the part needs to be flipped.

abstract.shortdesc: Fully aligns the selected solid body for milling in a single click.
abstract.p: Performs a complete 3-phase alignment: (1) finds the best Z axis from planar and cylindrical face areas, (2) orients XY so the longest flat edge aligns with X, (3) moves the origin to the centre-top of the bounding box.

abstract.shortdesc: Rotates the document around Z to align the selected edge or face with the X axis.
abstract.p: Select a linear edge, a planar face, or a wireframe element before clicking. The document rotates so that the selected geometry aligns with the X axis. If the geometry is already aligned, the document rotates 90° on each click.

abstract.shortdesc: Opens a dialog to place P0 at any corner, edge midpoint, or centre of the bounding box.
abstract.p: Choose a point on the 3×3 XY grid and a Z level (Z+, centre, or Z−). P0 is moved to the corresponding position on the selected solid's axis-aligned bounding box.

abstract.shortdesc: Applies a compensation preset to the selected contouring operations and validates lead-in/out compatibility.
abstract.p: Choose a preset from the drop-down: Profile Compensation, Center Compensation, or No Compensation. The tool checks that lead-in/out types and distances are compatible with the chosen compensation mode and reports any issues in the Event Window.

abstract.shortdesc: Sets compensation to "Profile" on the selected contouring operations.
abstract.p: Activates profile (cutter radius) compensation and validates lead-in/out entries — tangent types are incompatible with compensation. Also checks that lead distances exceed the tool radius.

abstract.shortdesc: Sets compensation to "Center" (tool center) on the selected contouring operations.
abstract.p: Activates tool-center compensation and validates lead-in/out compatibility. Reports any tangent entries or insufficient distances in the Event Window.

abstract.shortdesc: Removes compensation from the selected contouring operations.
abstract.p: Sets the machine compensation to None.

abstract.shortdesc: Sets the Open Edge property to False on all eligible sub-items of the selected FeatureChains.
abstract.p: Select one or more FeatureChains before clicking. Each sub-item whose Open Edge property is True will be closed. A summary of processed chains and closed edges is written to the Event Window.
